_meta.title: Tech Predictions That Never Happened
_meta.note: Confident, dated technology predictions made between 1940 and 2026 — predictions that named a year, the year came, and the prediction did or didn't land. Schema mirrors agi-forecast-lab; categories and outcome scoring are tech-prediction-specific. URLs are null where the primary source isn't independently verified yet — those entries should be promoted to verified_level=high in v2 after a sourcing pass.

rows.quote: A Tesla car next year will probably be 90 percent capable of autopilot.
rows.outcome_note: Tesla released Autopilot v1 in 2015; far short of '90% capable.'

rows.quote: I think we'll be able to do fully autonomous within 3 years.
rows.outcome_note: FSD remained Level 2 in 2018 and through 2026.

rows.quote: Tesla will be able to drive from LA to NY by end of 2017, fully autonomous.
rows.outcome_note: Promised demo never occurred.

rows.quote: By the middle of next year, we will have over a million Tesla cars on the road with full self-driving hardware.
rows.outcome_note: Hardware shipped; full self-driving did not.

rows.quote: I feel very confident predicting autonomous robotaxis for Tesla next year. Not in all jurisdictions but in some.
rows.outcome_note: No autonomous robotaxis shipped in 2020.

rows.quote: I'm extremely confident that Tesla will have level 5 next year.
rows.outcome_note: Level 5 not achieved.

rows.quote: FSD will be solved this year.
rows.outcome_note: FSD remained supervised L2.

rows.quote: Cybercab unsupervised in Texas and California in 2025.
rows.outcome_note: As of May 2026 — limited Texas pilot announced; broad rollout had not happened.

rows.quote: Self-driving cars will be available for everyday use within 5 years.
rows.outcome_note: Waymo limited robotaxi service launched 2018 in Phoenix; not 'everyday use.'

rows.quote: Self-driving cars will be ready before my son turns 16.
rows.outcome_note: Waymo robotaxi was geo-fenced; consumer self-driving not ready.

rows.quote: Self-driving cars are really, really hard — but we'll have commercial service by year-end.
rows.outcome_note: Waymo One launched limited service Phoenix Dec 2018 — narrowly on time, geo-fenced.

rows.quote: Cruise will launch robotaxi service in San Francisco by year-end 2019.
rows.outcome_note: Cruise SF launch slipped multiple times; commercial rides did not begin in 2019.

rows.quote: Cruise commercial scaling beginning 2022.
rows.outcome_note: Cruise paused operations after Oct 2023 SF incident.

rows.quote: Uber will have a self-driving fleet of one million vehicles by 2020.
rows.outcome_note: Uber ATG sold to Aurora in 2020 after fatal Tempe AZ crash.

rows.quote: GM will deploy a self-driving ride-sharing fleet at scale by 2019.
rows.outcome_note: Cruise (GM subsidiary) launched limited service late; not at scale.

rows.quote: By 2020, we will be ready to bring multiple affordable, energy-efficient, fully autonomous-driving vehicles to market.
rows.outcome_note: Nissan ProPilot is L2; no consumer fully autonomous vehicle shipped.

rows.quote: Toyota will have an autonomous highway-driving car by Tokyo 2020 Olympics.
rows.outcome_note: Toyota's Teammate Advanced Drive (L2+) shipped 2021 in Japan; not 'autonomous.'

rows.quote: 100% autonomous cars by 2026; complete penetration thereafter.
rows.source_title: 'Autonomous Cars: Self-Driving the New Auto Industry Paradigm'
rows.outcome_note: Vast majority of new cars in 2026 are L0–L2.

rows.quote: Robotaxi platforms generating $260B in revenue annually by 2024.
rows.outcome_note: Total robotaxi revenue 2024 was a tiny fraction of $260B.

rows.quote: Up to 15% of new cars sold in 2030 could be fully autonomous.
rows.source_title: 'Ten ways autonomous driving could redefine the automotive world'
rows.outcome_note: Trajectory makes 15% by 2030 unlikely; revisit at 2030.

rows.quote: My oldest son is 11 right now. I am working really hard so he won't ever need to get a driver's license.
rows.outcome_note: Self-driving did not eliminate driver's licenses.

rows.quote: Fully autonomous cars are 4 years away.
rows.outcome_note: Fully autonomous consumer vehicles still not shipped at scale by 2020.

rows.quote: We're focusing on autonomous systems. It's the mother of all AI projects.
rows.outcome_note: Apple Project Titan was wound down in early 2024.

rows.quote: VR will be the next major computing platform within a decade.
rows.source_title: Facebook post on Oculus acquisition, March 2014
rows.outcome_note: VR is not the dominant computing platform a decade later.

rows.quote: Within the next 5 years or so... we will effectively transition from people seeing us as primarily being a social media company to being a metaverse company.
rows.outcome_note: Meta refocused on AI in 2024; Reality Labs revenue remains tiny vs ads.

rows.quote: By 2030, we expect a billion people in the metaverse.
rows.outcome_note: Horizon Worlds MAU was widely reported as <1M in 2022 and grew slowly.

rows.quote: VR/AR will be an $80B market by 2025.
rows.source_title: 'Virtual & Augmented Reality' Profiles in Innovation, Jan 2016
rows.outcome_note: Total VR/AR revenue ~$15-25B range in 2025 estimates.

rows.quote: VR will hit its first billion-dollar consumer year by 2018.
rows.outcome_note: Hardware revenue did cross $1B; software/content remained small.

rows.quote: Consumer VR will have its iPhone moment with the Rift launch.
rows.outcome_note: Rift CV1 launched 2016; no iPhone-scale moment followed.

rows.quote: Magic Leap will ship a consumer mixed-reality device in 2-3 years.
rows.outcome_note: Magic Leap One shipped 2018, sold ~6,000 units; not consumer-scale.

rows.quote: VR will be as big or bigger than smartphones within 5 years.
rows.outcome_note: VR install base ~10M vs smartphone install base ~5B in 2020.

rows.quote: Widespread VR consumer adoption within 5–6 years.
rows.outcome_note: First VR cycle collapsed by mid-1990s.

rows.quote: Within five years, all televisions will be 3D.
rows.outcome_note: 3D TV sales peaked 2012 then collapsed; major manufacturers dropped 3D by 2017.

rows.quote: 3D TVs will be 50% of TV sales by 2014.
rows.outcome_note: 3D TVs peaked at single-digit-percent share globally.

rows.quote: 3D TV penetration at 30% globally by 2015.
rows.outcome_note: Below 5% in most years; effectively 0% by 2017.

rows.quote: 3D will dominate movie release schedules by 2012.
rows.outcome_note: Major animated/tentpole films released in 3D, but 2D box office continued to dominate revenue.

rows.quote: ESPN 3D will be the future of sports broadcasting.
rows.outcome_note: ESPN 3D shut down in September 2013.

rows.quote: 3D will be a key pillar with majority adoption by 2013.
rows.outcome_note: Sony quietly dropped 3D from new TVs by 2017.

rows.quote: I think we'll see [Google Glass] on the streets ... within a year or so.
rows.outcome_note: Glass Explorer launched at $1,500; consumer version pulled in 2015.

rows.quote: Glass will be a broadly available consumer product by 2015.
rows.outcome_note: Consumer Glass program ended Jan 2015.

rows.quote: AR is going to be huge ... I'm so excited about it I just want to yell out and scream.
rows.outcome_note: Vision Pro shipped Feb 2024 — 5+ years after this prediction's implied timeline.

rows.quote: Apple to ship AR glasses in 2020.
rows.outcome_note: Pushed back ~4 times before Vision Pro shipped 2024 (which is not glasses).

rows.quote: Apple AR/VR headset by 2020.
rows.outcome_note: Vision Pro shipped Feb 2024 — 4 years late.

rows.quote: HoloLens consumer launch by 2018; the holographic computing era.
rows.outcome_note: HoloLens stayed enterprise-only; HoloLens 2 also enterprise.

rows.quote: Spectacles will reinvent how people share.
rows.outcome_note: Snap took $40M write-down on unsold v1 inventory; later versions remained niche.

rows.quote: Project Nazaré full AR glasses shipping by 2024.
rows.outcome_note: Orion prototype shown 2024 but not shipped.

rows.quote: If Bitcoin's not at $500K by end of 2020, I will eat my dick on national TV.
rows.outcome_note: BTC closed 2020 at ~$29K. McAfee died June 2021 in Spanish prison cell.

rows.quote: By 2022 or 2023... I think you'll see Bitcoin at $250,000.
Bitcoin will be over $10K by 2018.
rows.outcome_note: BTC crossed $10K in late 2017 then briefly hit $19K in Dec 2017 before fallling. Closed 2018 at ~$3.7K.

rows.quote: Bitcoin could be at $40K by end of 2018.
rows.outcome_note: BTC closed 2018 ~$3.7K.

rows.quote: Our base case for Bitcoin is roughly $600,000... and our bull case is $1.5 million.
rows.outcome_note: BTC peaked ~$108K early 2025; far from base case. Time horizon 2030.

rows.quote: The world will have a single currency, the internet will have a single currency. I personally believe that it will be Bitcoin.
rows.outcome_note: Multi-currency world persisted; no single internet currency emerged.

rows.quote: Bitcoin will be as fundamental as TCP/IP within a decade.
rows.outcome_note: Bitcoin became a major asset class; not 'as fundamental' as a base internet protocol.

rows.quote: Bitcoin Cash will surpass Bitcoin in market cap by 2020.
rows.outcome_note: BCH market cap was ~1% of BTC by end of 2020.

rows.quote: Bitcoin to $100K by end of 2021.
rows.outcome_note: BTC peaked at ~$69K in Nov 2021.

rows.quote: Ethereum scaling (sharding + plasma) production-ready by 2018-2020.
rows.outcome_note: The Merge happened Sept 2022; sharding roadmap evolved into rollups; full sharding pushed beyond 2024.

rows.quote: NFTs in Instagram/Facebook by 2024 as default.
rows.outcome_note: Meta wound down digital-collectibles features mid-2023.

rows.quote: Personal flying vehicles within 2-3 years.
rows.outcome_note: Kitty Hawk shut down in 2022 without shipping a consumer aircraft.

rows.quote: Mass production of flying cars by mid-2020s.
rows.outcome_note: Kitty Hawk dissolved; Thrun moved on; mass production not reached.

rows.quote: Commercial eVTOL service by 2023.
rows.outcome_note: FAA type certification pushed into 2025+; no commercial service.

rows.quote: Air taxi commercial service in Singapore by 2021.
rows.outcome_note: Volocopter filed for insolvency Dec 2024.

rows.quote: UberAir flying-taxi service in LA, Dallas, Dubai by 2023.
rows.outcome_note: Uber Elevate sold to Joby in Dec 2020.

rows.quote: Mark my word: a combination airplane and motorcar is coming. You may smile, but it will come.
rows.outcome_note: Still hasn't 'come' as a mass product 86 years later.

rows.quote: Moller Skycar production by 1995.
rows.outcome_note: Moller Skycar M400 made tethered flights but never shipped.

rows.quote: Google will achieve quantum supremacy by 2018.
rows.outcome_note: Achieved 2019; subsequently disputed by IBM scientists.

rows.quote: IBM will ship a 1000+-qubit quantum processor by 2023.
rows.outcome_note: IBM Condor (1121 qubits) announced Dec 2023.

rows.quote: A useful, fault-tolerant quantum computer by 2029.
rows.outcome_note: Time horizon 2029; status unknown as of 2026.

rows.quote: We'll see a shift from a mobile-first to an AI-first world.
rows.outcome_note: AI-first emerged via LLMs in 2023+; voice-replacing-screens did not happen.

rows.quote: Voice will be the dominant computing interface by 2020 — Alexa-skill ecosystem will rival the App Store.
rows.outcome_note: Alexa Skills count plateaued; Amazon laid off most Alexa team in 2023.

rows.quote: Voice queries will reach 50% of all searches by 2020.
rows.outcome_note: Voice search was estimated <20% of queries by 2020.

rows.quote: 50 billion connected IoT devices by 2024.
rows.outcome_note: Most credible 2024 estimates: 18-30 billion connected devices.

rows.quote: 25 billion connected things by 2020.
rows.outcome_note: 2020 estimates: 8-12 billion connected things.

rows.quote: Smart home ubiquitous in new construction by 2020.
rows.outcome_note: Smart-home features common but far from ubiquitous in new construction.

rows.quote: Year of the Linux Desktop
rows.outcome_note: Has been declared annually for 25+ years; never realized at meaningful market share.

rows.quote: Amazon Prime Air drone delivery within 5 years.
rows.outcome_note: Limited drone deliveries began in College Station, TX in 2022; not at scale.

rows.quote: Hyperloop SF-LA in 30 minutes within a decade.
rows.outcome_note: Boring Co. test tracks; no commercial Hyperloop. Virgin Hyperloop laid off most staff 2022.

rows.quote: Mobile internet usage will exceed desktop by 2010.
rows.outcome_note: Crossover happened 2014 globally; ~4 years late.

rows.quote: Streaming will displace DVD rental in 5 years.
rows.outcome_note: Streaming overtook DVD revenue ~2013; consumer streaming dominance ~2017. ~7 years late.

rows.quote: I think if you work as a radiologist you are like the coyote that's already over the edge of the cliff... we should stop training radiologists now.
rows.outcome_note: AI radiology tools became common 2023+; radiologists not displaced. Direction was right; magnitude and timing far off.

rows.quote: Computers will pass Turing-style benchmarks by 2029; reach human-level intelligence.
rows.outcome_note: Many Turing-style benchmarks crossed 2023-2025. Headline AGI claim still contested.

rows.quote: 100% EV new-car sales by 2025; ICE collapses.
rows.outcome_note: Global EV share 2025 ~25-30% of new car sales; some markets (Norway) over 90%, US ~10%.

rows.quote: Human-equivalent computing power available in supercomputers by 2010.
rows.outcome_note: Compute parity by Moravec's bio-anchor estimate roughly reached 2015-2020.
